text.title: Spec-Driven Debugging Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Spec-Driven Debugging. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Bug returns 3 sprints later under a different reviewer.
antipattern.fix: Add failing test BEFORE the fix; the test stays in the suite forever.

antipattern.symptom: Repro takes 200 lines; only 3 lines actually matter.
antipattern.fix: Apply delta-debugging or manual bisection; reduce to <=20 lines or single assertion.

antipattern.symptom: 3 hours spent speculating which commit introduced the regression.
antipattern.fix: `git bisect start <bad> <good>` + the failing spec as bisect oracle; finds the cause in O(log n) commits.

antipattern.symptom: Issue 'fix bug in checkout' closes 4 times; bug remains.
antipattern.fix: Bug-spec title MUST include input + observed + expected; one bug = one spec.
